overview.introduction: GitHub Actions is an API-driven automation platform that enables you to build, test, and deploy code directly from GitHub events.
overview.why_it_matters: Understanding GitHub Actions Fundamentals enables you to automate software checks and release packages directly from repository operations.
overview.industry_usage: Used by engineering teams globally to enforce test checks and deliver webapp packages automatically.
overview.what_you_will_build: A series of configured local Git repositories tracking YAML workflows, triggers, actions steps, and execution simulator logs.
learning_outcomes: Explain the workflow execution model of GitHub Actions Fundamentals.
Create workflows utilizing triggers and job steps.
Incorporate actions like actions/checkout and actions/upload-artifact.
Inject repository secrets variables securely inside workflow environment states.

key_concepts.title: Workflows, Jobs, & Steps
key_concepts.definition: A workflow is a configurable automated process. A workflow contains one or more jobs, and a job contains one or more sequential steps.
why_important: Locks sensitive secrets keys from logs displays.
key_concepts.explanation: When a workflow event is triggered, GitHub Actions spawns container host runners to execute listed script steps.

commands.name: on
commands.output_explanation: Triggers workflow runs when commits are pushed.

commands.name: uses
commands.output_explanation: Checks out active repository files inside workspace run directories.

key_concepts.pro_tip: Always specify explicit versions (e.g. @v4) for actions dependencies to lock pipeline builds states.
key_concepts.best_practices: Avoid executing commands with root/admin scopes in shell steps unless strictly required for configurations.
key_concepts.common_mistakes: Exposing production API keys directly in YAML files instead of using GitHub Secrets mappings.
key_concepts.real_world_usage: GitHub Actions automatically executes linter checks upon receiving a new Pull Request.
key_concepts.summary: GitHub Actions Fundamentals form the baseline of secure automated software pipelines.

interactive_examples.explanation: Create workflow directories and YAML targets.
interactive_examples.common_mistakes: Incorrect indentation structures in YAML workflow definitions.
interactive_examples.tips: Utilize 2-space indentation levels for YAML files formatting.

lessons.title: 1. Initial Check (Create workflow file)
lessons.instruction: Create directories '.github/workflows/' and a workflow YAML file named 'main.yml'.

lessons.title: 2. Check status
lessons.instruction: Run git status to see modified files.

lessons.title: 3. Audit Log History
lessons.explanation: Displays timeline log of previous commits.
lessons.instruction: Print commit history log to console screen.

lessons.title: 5. Show ref mappings
lessons.explanation: Resolve tags and branches SHA identifiers.
lessons.instruction: Query refs maps inside repository workspace.

lessons.title: 6. Show active branch
lessons.explanation: Queries branch name where checkout pointer HEAD is sitting.
lessons.instruction: List all branches using git branch.

lessons.title: 7. Audit reflog
lessons.explanation: Audits reflog containing history revisions of pointers checkout switches.

lessons.title: Mini Challenge: First Workflow
lessons.definition: No step-by-step instructions are provided here. Execute this task directly.
lessons.explanation: Complete this task on your own.
lessons.instruction: Author a workflow in '.github/workflows/main.yml' that runs on 'push' to 'main' branch, using 'ubuntu-latest', and prints a welcome message.
lessons.example:
echo -e 'name: Welcome Workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  welcome-job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Say Hello
        run: echo "Welcome to DevLab GitHub Actions!"' > .github/workflows/main.yml && git add .github/workflows/main.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add welcome workflow'
lessons.hint:
Run command: echo -e 'name: Welcome Workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  welcome-job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Say Hello
        run: echo "Welcome to DevLab GitHub Actions!"' > .github/workflows/main.yml && git add .github/workflows/main.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add welcome workflow'
lessons.solution:
echo -e 'name: Welcome Workflow
on: [push]
jobs:
  welcome-job:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Say Hello
        run: echo "Welcome to DevLab GitHub Actions!"' > .github/workflows/main.yml && git add .github/workflows/main.yml && git commit -m 'feat: add welcome workflow'

exercises.title: Configure Git attributes
exercises.problem: Practice git workspace command configurations by querying history logs.
exercises.objective: Verify GitHub Actions Fundamentals core specifications.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of GitHub Actions GitHub Actions Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: GitHub Actions automates compilation, testing, and deployment workflows directly from events.

quiz.question: Where must workflow configuration files be located in a repository?
quiz.explanation: GitHub Actions searches for workflow YAML files inside the '.github/workflows/' directory.

quiz.question: What is the trigger keyword in a workflow definition?
quiz.explanation: The 'on' keyword specifies the event triggers (like push or pull_request) that run the workflow.

quiz.question: Which action checks out repository code inside a workflow?
quiz.explanation: The 'actions/checkout' action is the standard utility used to check out repository code.

quiz.question: Which keyword specifies the OS environment type for a runner?
quiz.explanation: The 'runs-on' parameter specifies the runner operating system context (e.g. ubuntu-latest).

quiz.question: What keyword defines custom script actions to run in terminal shells?
quiz.explanation: The 'run' keyword runs command-line scripts using the runner's shell environment.

quiz.question: What runs steps in a workflow concurrently or sequentially?
quiz.explanation: Jobs inside a workflow run in parallel by default, while steps inside a job run sequentially.

quiz.question: How do you share build files between jobs or save them after workflows finish?
quiz.explanation: Artifacts allow you to persist and share data across workflow jobs and runs.

quiz.question: Where should encryption passwords or deployment keys be saved?
quiz.explanation: Encrypted secrets store credentials securely within GitHub Actions without printing them in logs.

quiz.question: What action is used to save build assets in a workflow?
quiz.explanation: The 'actions/upload-artifact' action uploads files from your runner workspace to GitHub.

resources.summary: Learn more about GitHub Actions GitHub Actions Fundamentals through official documentation, guides, and cheat sheets.

links.title: GitHub Actions GitHub Actions Fundamentals Documentation Guide
links.description: Official documentation and manual for GitHub Actions Fundamentals.

links.title: GitHub Actions Cheat Sheet
links.description: Quick reference guide for common workflow keys and triggers.

resources.cheat_sheet:
on: [push]                  # Trigger on push
runs-on: ubuntu-latest       # Use Ubuntu runner
uses: actions/checkout@v4    # Import Checkout action
run: npm run test            # Execute shell test script
revision_notes: Workflows are configured as YAML configurations in .github/workflows/.
Secrets are referenced utilizing ${{ secrets.KEY }} expressions.
Jobs execute inside isolated runner containers.
best_practices: Limit runner scopes and clean up caches dynamically.
Store credentials securely inside Encrypted Secrets.
Audit logs outputs to avoid printing private tokens.
beginner_mistakes: Using tabs instead of 2-space indentation levels inside YAML templates.
Adding hardcoded password strings in workflow files.

interview_questions.question: What is GitHub Actions GitHub Actions Fundamentals?
interview_questions.answer: It represents the continuous integration and automation runner platform for GitHub Actions Fundamentals.

books.title: Automating workflows with GitHub Actions — Scott Chacon
books.description: Comprehensive reference guide explaining runner internals and pipelines configuration.
